summary: express-initial postinstall hook downloads and executes a remote payload
details: package.json declares `"postinstall": "node index.js"`, which auto-runs on `npm install`. index.js is heavily RC4+base64 obfuscated (245-entry rotated string array with two decoders) and, once decoded, performs an HTTP GET to a runtime-derived URL, writes the response body to a file in the current working directory via `fs.writeFileSync(..., {flag:'w+'})`, then executes the dropped file with `child_process.execSync(..., {windowsHide:true, cwd:process.cwd()})`. A top-level `process.on('uncaughtException', () => {})` swallows errors so the install appears to succeed even if the dropper fails. The package name borrows the `express` brand, ships empty `author` and `description` fields, and the README itself describes the script as 'heavily obfuscated' and warns to run it only inside Docker/VM — none of which is consistent with a legitimate utility. Any developer or CI machine that runs `npm install express-initial` will fetch and execute attacker-controlled code under the installing user's account.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
